speaker: RUTLAND
text: Ah, whither shall I fly to scape their hands?

speaker: RUTLAND
text: Ah, tutor, look where bloody Clifford comes.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Chaplain, away. Thy priesthood saves thy life.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: As for the brat of this accursèd duke,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Whose father slew my father, he shall die.

speaker: TUTOR
text: And I, my lord, will bear him company.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Soldiers, away with him.

speaker: TUTOR
text: Ah, Clifford, murder not this innocent child,

speaker: TUTOR
text: Lest thou be hated both of God and man.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: How now? Is he dead already? Or is it fear

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: That makes him close his eyes? I’ll open them.

speaker: RUTLAND
text: So looks the pent-up lion o’er the wretch

speaker: RUTLAND
text: That trembles under his devouring paws;

speaker: RUTLAND
text: And so he walks, insulting o’er his prey;

speaker: RUTLAND
text: And so he comes to rend his limbs asunder.

speaker: RUTLAND
text: Ah, gentle Clifford, kill me with thy sword

speaker: RUTLAND
text: And not with such a cruel threat’ning look.

speaker: RUTLAND
text: Sweet Clifford, hear me speak before I die.

speaker: RUTLAND
text: I am too mean a subject for thy wrath.

speaker: RUTLAND
text: Be thou revenged on men, and let me live.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: In vain thou speak’st, poor boy. My father’s blood

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Hath stopped the passage where thy words should enter.

speaker: RUTLAND
text: Then let my father’s blood open it again;

speaker: RUTLAND
text: He is a man and, Clifford, cope with him.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Had I thy brethren here, their lives and thine

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Were not revenge sufficient for me.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: No, if I digged up thy forefathers’ graves

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And hung their rotten coffins up in chains,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: It could not slake mine ire nor ease my heart.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: The sight of any of the house of York

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Is as a fury to torment my soul,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And till I root out their accursèd line

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And leave not one alive, I live in hell.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Therefore—

speaker: RUTLAND
text: O, let me pray before I take my death!

speaker: RUTLAND
text: To thee I pray: sweet Clifford, pity me!

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Such pity as my rapier’s point affords.

speaker: RUTLAND
text: I never did thee harm. Why wilt thou slay me?

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Thy father hath.

speaker: RUTLAND
text: But ’twas ere I was born.

speaker: RUTLAND
text: Thou hast one son; for his sake pity me,

speaker: RUTLAND
text: Lest in revenge thereof, sith God is just,

speaker: RUTLAND
text: He be as miserably slain as I.

speaker: RUTLAND
text: Ah, let me live in prison all my days,

speaker: RUTLAND
text: And when I give occasion of offense

speaker: RUTLAND
text: Then let me die, for now thou hast no cause.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: No cause? Thy father slew my father; therefore die.

speaker: RUTLAND
text: Di faciant laudis summa sit ista tuae!

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Plantagenet, I come, Plantagenet!

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And this thy son’s blood, cleaving to my blade,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Shall rust upon my weapon till thy blood,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Congealed with this, do make me wipe off both.
